title: ELI Internship Application
html:
TL;DR

  • January 2023 application essays for the OSU Emerging Leaders Internship program — Yahir applying as a junior with a crypto focus
  • Origin story for the crypto interest: high-school internet-rabbit-hole on "programmable internet money" — frames it as financial freedom tooling for unstable countries
  • Lists current crypto credentials: founding Corvallis Crypto Club from scratch, financial sector lead at OSIG, pre-IPO internship at Dutch Bros
  • Dream-employer list: Coinbase (uncompromised values, talent hub) and Block Inc. (mobile merchant payments in rural Africa)
  • Self-differentiator framing: "originality + initiative" — explicitly attacks "crowd thought" as evolutionary holdover gone bad
  • "Excessively net positive person" articulated for the first time as the life mission

Notes

OSU's Emerging Leaders Internship application — five short essays. Yahir's pitch positions crypto as the intersection of his finance interest (OSIG financial sector lead) and his big-picture mission of financial freedom for people in unstable monetary regimes.

Three of the framings here become recurring vocabulary across 2023:

  1. "Excessively net positive person" — the life-mission phrase. First time it appears verbatim.
  2. Originality vs crowd-thought — "the majority of people will choose whatever is the most popular thing the majority of the time, even if it's not in their best interest; this has previously helped the human species survive but is now a dangerous pitfall." The intellectual seed of the Be Your Own Original concept.
  3. Company-culture-as-heartbeat — credited to the Dutch Bros pre-IPO internship; he says this was the thing he most wants to bring forward into his own ventures.

Dream employers: Coinbase for values + talent density; Block Inc. for the rural-Africa mobile-merchant payment work — "staying only in the American bubble would be a regretful career decision."

Connects to: 2023 03 24 thoughts and things 32422 (six weeks later — AI panic re-frames the crypto thesis), corvallis crypto club.
